plan: 1. Create CSS design tokens as CSS custom properties in `src/ui/tokens.css` — define color palette (brand colors, tier colors: green/amber/blue), spacing scale, typography scale, and responsive breakpoints (mobile: <768px, tablet: 768-1024px, desktop: >1024px). Import in main.tsx.

2. Create `src/ui/Layout/Layout.tsx` and `src/ui/Layout/Layout.module.css` — a responsive shell component with three semantic regions: `<header>`, `<nav>`, and `<main>`. Use CSS Grid or Flexbox for layout. On mobile, stack header/nav vertically with nav collapsible or horizontal scroll. On desktop, use full-width header with navigation alongside main content area. Ensure `overflow-x: hidden` on the viewport and `max-width: 100vw` to prevent horizontal scroll.

3. Create `src/ui/Header/Header.tsx` and `src/ui/Header/Header.module.css` — renders site title ('Open Hand') in a `<header>` element with appropriate semantic heading. Style to match 'warm, premium, calm' aesthetic. Responsive: fixed or sticky on mobile, standard flow on desktop.

4. Create `src/ui/Nav/Nav.tsx` and `src/ui/Nav/Nav.module.css` — renders navigation links (Home, Browse, Play, Guide) inside a `<nav>` element with `aria-label`. On mobile: horizontal bar or bottom nav. On desktop: horizontal top nav or sidebar. All links are placeholder anchors for now (no router yet) — use `<a href>` or `<button>` elements with visible focus states.

5. Update `src/App.tsx` to import and compose the Layout shell: wrap existing content with `<Layout>` containing `<Header>` and `<Nav>`. The main content area renders a placeholder (e.g., 'Welcome to Open Hand') that future feature pages will replace.
6. Update `src/ui/index.ts` barrel file to re-export Layout, Header, and Nav components.

7. Write unit tests in `tests/unit/layout-shell.test.tsx` — verify: header element is rendered, nav element is rendered with navigation links, main content area is rendered, all regions are accessible (roles, labels). Use @testing-library/react.

8. Write responsive viewport tests in `tests/e2e/responsive-shell.spec.ts` — Playwright tests at mobile (375px), tablet (768px), and desktop (1280px) viewports: verify no horizontal scrollbar (`document.documentElement.scrollWidth <= window.innerWidth`), header/nav/main are visible, layout remains usable.
9. Run quality gates: `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test` to ensure all pass. Fix any issues.
10. Run `npm run test:e2e` to verify Playwright responsive tests pass across viewports.
testing_recommendations: Unit test: Header component renders a <header> element with site title
Unit test: Nav component renders a <nav> element with aria-label and expected navigation links (Home, Browse, Play, Guide)
Unit test: Layout component renders header, nav, and main content regions
Unit test: Main content area renders children passed to it
E2E test: At mobile viewport (375px), no horizontal scrolling occurs (scrollWidth <= innerWidth)
E2E test: At tablet viewport (768px), header, nav, and main are all visible
E2E test: At desktop viewport (1280px), layout is usable — all regions visible and appropriately sized
E2E test: Navigation links are keyboard-focusable with visible focus indicators
Verify npm run lint passes with no errors
Verify npm run typecheck passes with no errors
risks: No router is installed yet — navigation links will be placeholder anchors; must ensure they don't break when routing is added in a future ticket
CSS Modules approach needs to be validated with the existing Vite config (should work out of the box but verify)
Responsive breakpoints are being established for the first time — choices here will constrain all future UI work, so they should follow standard conventions
Existing App.test.tsx asserts an h1 with 'Card Deck v2' — updating App.tsx will require updating this test to match new content
No design mockups exist — the 'warm, premium, calm' aesthetic is subjective and may need iteration
